column3: Bedrock and boulders with a rare patch of muddy sand, in upper and lower infralittoral, -2.5 to -20.5m. Thick Laminaria hyperborea park below this. Thick understorey of foliose red algae, and rather undiverse, if rich, stipe flora. Boltenia echinata frequent on the kelp stipes. Characteristically circalittoral species such as Caryophyllia smithii, Terebratulina retusa and Diazona violacea all present. Large growths of Ascidia mentula common, as was Antedon bifida.

column2: Chondria tenuissima has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value. Extensive smooth bedrock dominated by encrusting brown algae with vertical and some overhanging surfaces with boulders between and fringing the muddy gravel slope. Dominated at shallow depths by dense patches of Brongniartella byssoides with Bonnemaisonia asparagoides and sparse Laminara hyperborea and L. saccharina. Deeper rocks grazed by Echinus esculentus but with Parasmittina trispinosa and Alcyonium digitatum characterising vertical or overhanging surfaces. Boulders on/near to the sediment dominated by Balanus crenatus and Rhizocaulus verticillatus in places.

An interesting physical/topographical feature, of obscure origin.

column2: Infralittoral bedrock dominated by Ophiothrix fragilis with occasional Lithothamnion spp. and sparse Laminaria saccharina. Horizontal fissures with walls dominated by Pomatoceros triqueter and with Thorogobius ephippiatus and Galathea strigosa within.
column3: Bedrock and boulders with a rare patch of muddy sand, in upper and lower infralittoral, -2.5 to -20.5m. Thick Laminaria hyperborea park below this. Thick understorey of foliose red algae, and rather undiverse, if rich, stipe flora. Boltenia echinata frequent on the kelp stipes. Characteristically circalittoral species such as Caryophyllia smithii, Terebratulina retusa and Diazona violacea all present. Large growths of Ascidia mentula common, as was Antedon bifida.

column2: Stepped steeply-sloping bedrock at 20 to 22 m BCD dominated mby encrusting Corallinaceae (90%) and encrusting dark red algae (10%) covered overall by Ophiothrix fragilis with fairly rich variety of species.

column2: Vertical and overhanging surfaces including a cave formed by the overhang at c. 5 to 11 m BCD. Colonised by a large variety of animals with Ascidia mentula and Sabella pavonina particularly conspicuous. Several rarely encountered species present.
column3: Lower circalittoral of steep bedrock from -24.5 m followed by boulders on bedrock, a fairly level plain of boulders with muddy sand between at -41.5 m. Fauna typical of slight shelter, with frequent Swiftia pallida, Terebratulina retusa and Iophionopsis nigrescens, and occasional Axinella infundibuliformis. Neocrania anomala also frequent.

column2: Near horizontal plain of very soft mud with small quantity of shell fragments. Surface slightly uneven with rare burrows. Extensive clumps of Sabella pavonina with abundant Ascidiella aspersa and Antedon bifida on the Sabella pavonina. Mud surface festooned with small worm tubes (3-4mm) above surface with isolated individuals of; Thyone fusus, Amphitrite sp., Myxicola infundibulum, Mya truncata, Virgularia mirabilis, Sabella pavonina, clumps of Ascidiella aspersa with frequent Amphilectus fucorum and Halichondria bowerbanki, which in turn supported Leucosolinia sp.
column3: Rhodophycota has been removed from the species list for this record as more specific related taxa were also present, these are now marked as characterising. Flat muddy plain, with boulders close to the bedrock cliff. Algal cover especially foliose reds.

column2: Bedrock and boulders with a rare patch of muddy sand, in upper and lower infralittoral, -2.5 to -20.5m. Thick Laminaria hyperborea park below this. Thick understorey of foliose red algae, and rather undiverse, if rich, stipe flora. Boltenia echinata frequent on the kelp stipes. Characteristically circalittoral species such as Caryophyllia smithii, Terebratulina retusa and Diazona violacea all present. Large growths of Ascidia mentula common, as was Antedon bifida.
column3: Bedrock with steep face covered in dense Mytilus edulis and horizontal upper surface with Laminaria saccharina and Halidrys siliquosa.

column2: Chondria tenuissima has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value. Extensive smooth bedrock dominated by encrusting brown algae with verticl and some overhanging surfaces with boulders between and fringing the muddy gravel slope. Dominated at shallow depths by dense patches of Brongniartella byssoides with Bonnemaisonia asparagoides and sparse Laminaria hyperborea and L. saccharina. Deeper rocks grazed by Echinus esculentus but with Parasmittina trispinosa and Alcyonium digitatum characterising vertical or overhanging surfaces. Boulders on/near to the sediment dominated by Balanus crenatus and Rhizocaulus verticillatus in places.
column3: Chondria tenuissima has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value. Deeper rocks grazed by Echinus esculentus but with Parasmittina trispinosa and Alcyonium digitatum characterising vertical or overhanging surfaces. Boulders on/near to the sediment dominated by Balanus crenatus and Rhizocaulus verticillatus in places.

column2: Rhodophycota indet.(non-calc.crusts) has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value. A vertical bedrock wall heavily urchin grazed. column3: Rhodophycota indet.(non-calc.crusts), Plagiospora has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value. column2: Near horizontal plain of very soft mud with small quantity of shell fragments. Surface slightly uneven with rare burrows. Extensive clumps of Sabella pavonina with abundant Ascidiella aspersa and Antedon bifida on the Sabella pavonina. Mud surface festooned with small worm tubes (3-4mm) above surface with isolated individuals of; Thyone fusus, Amphitrite sp., Myxicola infundibulum, Mya truncata, Virgularia mirabilis, Sabella pavonina, clumps of Ascidiella aspersa with frequent Amphilectus fucorum and Halichondria bowerbanki, which in turn supported Leucosolinia sp.
column3: Lower infralittoral and upper circalittoral cobbles, pebbles and shell gravel from 9 m to 15 m. Delessera sanguinea and Phycodrys rubens , Phyllophora crispa and Dilsea carnosa common, with various other red algae, Enteromorpha spp. and Ulva spp., Pomatoceros triqueter, Pododesmus patelliformis, Galathea intermedia, prawns and shrimps and two-spotted gobies and terebellids. Dendrodoa grossularia common. Laminaria saccharina to 12 m BSL. The seabed was a dense carpet of foliose algae with drift green algae intermixed and only a few small bare patches of sediment with Amphitrite spp.

column2: Large boulders and bedrock outcrops on muddy sediments shallower than 22 m BCD. Characterised by a wide variety of animal species but with Swiftia pallida, Diazonia violacea frequent.
column3: Steep bank of small boulders from -2 m to -11 m, covered in extremely dense blanket of Laminaria saccharina. Much of the kelp was not very firmly attached to the boulders. Low species diversity because the kelp was so thick that nothing grew on the stipes, and the rock was too far below the plant cover for light to penetrate. An interesting physical/topographical feature, of obscure origin.

Mud excavated more extensively above 18m.
column3: Ensis has been removed from the species list for this record as more specific related taxa were also present, these are now marked as characterising. Foliose red algae has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value. column3: Bedrock and boulders with a rare patch of muddy sand, in upper and lower infralittoral, -2.5 to -20.5m. Thick Laminaria hyperborea park below this. Characteristically circalittoral species such as Caryophyllia smithii, Terebratulina retusa and Diazona violacea all present. Large growths of Ascidia mentula common, as was Antedon bifida.

column2: Silted rock largely bare below the silt with sparse attached flora and fauna including occasional spots of encrusting Corallinacea and colonised by scattered worm/amphipod tubes. Dendrodoa grossularia, Lepidochitona cinerea, Polymastia mamillaris and filamentous red algae etc.
column3: Chondria tenuissima has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value. Extensive smooth bedrock dominated by encrusting brown algae with vertical and some overhanging surfaces with boulders between and fringing the muddy gravel slope. Dominated at shallow depths by dense patches of Brongniartella byssoides with Bonnemaisonia asparagoides and sparse Laminaria hyperborea and L. saccharina. Deeper rocks grazed by Echinus esculentus but with Parasmittina trispinosa and Alcyonium digitatum characterising vertical or overhanging surfaces. Boulders on/near to the sediment dominated by Balanus crenatus and Rhizocaulus verticillatus in places.

column2: Steep bank ofp small boulders from -2 m to -11 m, covered in extremely dense blanket of Laminaria saccharina. Much of the kelp was not very firmly attached to the boulders. Low species diversity because the kelp was so thick that nothing grew on the stipes, and the rock was too far below the plant cover for light to penetrate. An interesting physical/topographical feature, of obscure origin.

column2: Chondria tenuissima has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value. Extensive smooth bedrock dominated by encrusting brown algae with vertical and some overhanging surfaces with boulders between and fringing the muddy gravel slope. Dominated at shallow depths by dense patches of Brongniartella byssoides with Bonnemaisonia asparagoides and sparse Laminaria hyperborea and L. saccharina. Deeper rocks grazed by Echinus esculentus but with Parasmittina trispinosa and Alcyonium digitatum characterising vertical or overhanging surfaces. Boulders on/ear to the sediment dominated by Balanus crenatus and Rhizocaulus verticillatus in places.

column2: Chondria tenuissima has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value. Extensive smooth bedrock dominated by encrusting brown algae with vertical and some overhanging surfaces with boulders between and fringing the muddy gravel slope. Dominated at shallow depths by dense patches of Brongniartella byssoides with Bonnemaisonia asparagqoides and sparse Laminaria hyperborea and L. saccharina. Deeper rocks grazed by Echinus esculentus but with Parasmittina trispinosa and Alcyonium digitatum characterising vertical or overhanging surfaces. Boulders on/near to the sediment dominated by Balanus crenatus and Rhizocaulus verticillatus in places.

column2: Vertical and overhanging surfaces including a cave formed by the overhang at c. 5 to 11 m BCD. Colonised by a large variety of animals with Ascidia mentula and Sabella pavonina particularly conspicuous. Several rarely encountered species present.
column3: A plain of coarse sediment (muddy gravel) with cobbles and pebbles and a few small boulders and shells. Dense stands of Laminaria saccharina with Chorda filum and filamentous browns and foliose reds growing profusely in clearings. Sabella pavonina common amongst algae, and a few Modiolus modiolus present. Large clearings of sediment with Chone spp. and occasional Branchiomma bombyx.

column2: Bedrock in upper circalittoral, -20.5 to -24.5. Red algae not profuse, but present, with Brongniartella byssoides and Rhodophyllis divaricata both frequent. Animal life much as in lower circalittoral (see Habitat 3).
column3: Gently sloping mud with dead shells becoming softer with less shell below 4 m BCD. Depth band surveyed from 2.5 - 6.5 m BCD. Philine aperta common, Virgularia mirabilis present.

Extensive clumps of Sabella pavonina with abundant Ascidiella aspersa and Antedon bifida on the Sabella pavonina. Mud surface festooned with small worm tubes (3-4mm) above surface with isolated individuals of; Thyone fusus, Amphitrite sp., Myxicola infundibulum, Mya truncata, Virgularia mirabilis, Sabella pavonina, clumps of Ascidiella aspersa with frequent Amphilectus fucorum and Halichondria bowerbanki, which in turn supported Leucosolinia sp.

column2: Channel floor of cobbles, boulder and pebbles, highly tide scoured. Dominated by hydroids and broyzoans, particularly Sertularia argentea, Hydrallmania falcata, Eucratea loricata and Vesicularia spinosa.
column3: Channel floor of cobbles, boulder and pebbles, highly tide scoured. Dominated by hydroids and bryozoans, particularly Sertularia argentea, Hydrallmania falcata, Eucratea loricata and Vesicularia spinosa.

column2: A plain of coarse sediment (muddy gravel) with cobbles and pebbles and a few small boulders and shells. Dense stands of Laminaria saccharina with Chorda filum and filamentous browns and foliose reds growing profusely in clearings. Sabella pavonina common amongst algae, and a few Modiolus modiolus present. Large clearings of sediment with Chone spp. and occasional Branchiomma bombyx.
column3: Chondria tenuissima has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value. Extensive smooth bedrock dominated by encrusting brown algae with vertical and some overhanging surfaces with boulders between and fringing the muddy gravel slope. Dominated at shallow depths by dense patches of Brongniartella byssoides with Bonnemaisonia asparagoides and sparse Laminaria hyperborea and L. saccharina. Deeper rocks grazed by Echinus esculentus but with Parasmittina trispinosa and Alcyonium digitatum characterising vertical or overhanging surfaces. Boulders on/near to the sediment dominated by Balanus crenatus and Rhizocaulus verticillatus in places.

column2: Boulder tumble at 9 - 10 m dominated (60%) by encrusting Corallinacea and (40%) encrusting dark red Rhodophycota with common Tectura virginea. Abundant Laminaria saccharina overall with scattered foliose red algae in patches.
column3: Plain of wave-marked coarse shell gravel, characterised by Neopentadactyla mixta and Ensis spp., with large numbers of Pomatoschistus pictus. Foliose algae attached to shells or drift in furrows. Eggs of Natica spp. present.

Extensive smooth bedrock dominated by encrusting brown algae with vertical and some overhanging surfaces with boulders between and fringing the muddy gravel slope. Dominated at shallow depths by dense patches of Brongniartella byssoides with Bonnemaisonia asparagoides and sparse Laminaria hyperborea and L. saccharina. Deeper rocks grazed by Echinus esculentus but with Parasmittina trispinosa and Alcyonium digitatum characterising vertical or overhanging surfaces. Boulders on/near to the sediment dominated by Balanus crenatus and Rhizocaulus verticillatus in places.

column2: Steep slope of stable clay mud with many dead Turritella communis. A lot of Nephrops norvegicus burrows.
column3: Muddy slope with shell debris and cobbles with much filamentous algae. Diatom mat on the sediment surface. Algae petered out at 16 m and Pennatula phosphorea became more numerous.

column2: A plain of coarse sediment (muddy gravel) with cobbles and pebbles and a few small boulders and shells. Dense stands of Laminaria saccharina with Chorda filum and filamentous browns and foliose reds growing profusely in clearings. Sabella pavonina common amongst algae, and a few Modiolus modiolus present. Large clearings of sediment with Chone spp. and occasional Branchiomma bombyx.
